hypothesis: If models trained with equivariance-inducing regularization (such as equigrad) achieve representational alignment comparable to architecturally equivariant models despite lower computational cost, then systematically interpolating the strength of equivariance-inducing regularization during training will produce a monotonic increase in cross-model representational alignment that saturates at a threshold regularization strength corresponding to the point of diminishing returns in energy prediction accuracy, thereby establishing regularization strength as a quantitative, architecture-agnostic knob for controlling convergence toward the universal matter representation without requiring full architectural equivariance.
